info.title: trtc builder
info.summary: HTTP job broker that turns ONNX models into TensorRT engines on GPU hardware.
info.description:
The builder is a deliberately simple job broker: **one job = one tar = one engine back**.

## Workflow

1. Lay out `trtc_build_spec.json` next to the ONNX file (and any external data files) it references, and tar them up — the exact on-disk layout, no leading directories:

   ```sh
   tar cf job.tar trtc_build_spec.json model.onnx model.onnx.data
   ```

2. `POST /builds` with the tar as the body (or JSON `{"input_url": …}` pointing at one). You get a job `id` back immediately (`202`).

3. Poll `GET /builds/{id}` until `state` is `succeeded` or `failed`. Pass back the `log_offset` from each response to stream the build log incrementally.

4. Fetch the engine from `GET /builds/{id}/artifacts` — or skip this step by passing `output_url=<presigned PUT URL>` at submission time and the builder uploads the engine there itself.

```sh
curl -X POST -H 'Content-Type: application/x-tar' \
    --data-binary @job.tar "http://builder:8080/builds"
# -> {"id":"a1b2c3d4e5f6","state":"queued"}

curl "http://builder:8080/builds/a1b2c3d4e5f6"
curl -o model.engine "http://builder:8080/builds/a1b2c3d4e5f6/artifacts"
```

## The build spec

Every build option lives in `trtc_build_spec.json` inside the tar — nothing travels as CLI, query, or header parameters. See the `BuildSpec` schema. When this document is served by a TensorRT-linked builder (`GET /openapi.json`), the `BuilderConfig` option enums are generated from the same tables the build applies — they list exactly what that image's TensorRT accepts.

## Validation, in two layers

1. **Structure, at POST time** — tar members must be regular files with safe single-segment names; the spec must exist, be version 1, single-component, and every file it references must be in the tar. Violations are a `400` with the reason.
2. **Options, at job start** — only the TensorRT-linked build process knows what its TensorRT supports. A bad option fails the *job* within a second, with the list of known names in the job log and `error`.

## Caching

Engines and TensorRT timing caches persist under `TRTC_DATA_DIR`. Identical jobs (same file hashes + spec + TensorRT + GPU architecture) are served from the engine cache and succeed in seconds.

## Authentication

When the server is started with `TRTC_TOKEN` set, every request must carry `Authorization: Bearer <token>`; otherwise no auth is required.

servers.description: A running builder instance (e.g. the ghcr.io/dialohq/trtc-builder image).

tags.name: builds
tags.description: Submit build jobs, poll their status, and download engines.

post.description:
Queues a build. The body is either:

- **a job tar** (any non-JSON content type; plain, gzip, or zstd compressed) containing `trtc_build_spec.json` plus the ONNX and external data files it references, side by side, or
- **JSON** `{"input_url": "<presigned GET URL>"}` pointing at such a tar. The tar is then fetched (and validated) when the job starts, so a malformed remote tar fails the *job*, not this request.

A tar sent inline is validated and unpacked immediately, so malformed submissions fail this request with a `400` instead of failing the job.

Jobs run one at a time, in submission order.

parameters.name: output_url
parameters.description: Presigned PUT URL. When the build succeeds, the builder uploads the engine there (`Content-Type: application/octet-stream`) before marking the job `succeeded`. A rejected upload fails the job.

schema.description: A job tar: trtc_build_spec.json + the ONNX (+ external data files), no leading directories. Gzip- and zstd-compressed tars are accepted too.

input_url.description: Presigned GET URL of a job tar, fetched when the job starts.

400.description: Malformed submission: not a tar, unsafe member names, missing or invalid trtc_build_spec.json, files referenced by the spec missing from the tar, JSON without input_url, or an empty body.

get.description:
The full job status, including an incremental slice of the build log.

To stream the log while polling, pass the `log_offset` returned by the previous response: `log` then contains only the bytes appended since, and `log_offset` is the new end-of-log position.

Once `state` is `succeeded`, `result` holds the build manifest — the spec echoed back plus build facts (engine hash and size, file hashes, TensorRT version, GPU).

parameters.name: log_offset
parameters.description: Byte offset into the job log to resume from (from the previous response's log_offset).

value.error: build command exited with 1 (see job log)
value.log:
builder_config.flags: this TensorRT has no 'FP16' (known: DEBUG, ...)
ERROR: build command exited with 1 (see job log)

get.description: The serialized TensorRT engine, as raw bytes. Available only once the job has succeeded. Engines are only loadable by the same TensorRT version (and, unless built hardware-compatible, GPU architecture) reported by /info.

409.description: The job has not (or not successfully) produced an engine yet.

get.description: GPU facts, the trtc and TensorRT versions of this image, the number of jobs on disk, and the cache location. Clients use `tensorrt` to refuse a builder that does not match their lock pin.

This OpenAPI document, served by the running binary itself. BuilderConfig option enums are generated from the same compile-time tables the build applies, so they list exactly the names this image's TensorRT accepts — the contract cannot drift from the code.

bearerAuth.description: Required on every request when the server is started with TRTC_TOKEN; no authentication otherwise.

Unauthorized.description: Missing or wrong bearer token (only when TRTC_TOKEN is set).

created_at.description: Unix time (seconds) the job was accepted.

started_at.description: Unix time the build started. Absent while queued.

finished_at.description: Unix time the job reached a terminal state. Absent until then.

spec.description: The validated build spec from the job tar. For input_url jobs it appears once the tar has been fetched.

input_url.description: Where the job tar is fetched from (JSON submissions only).

output_url.description: Where the engine is uploaded on success, if requested.

error.description: Why the job failed (failed jobs only). Details are in the log.

result.description: The build manifest — present once state is succeeded, null or absent otherwise.

log.description: Job log bytes starting at the requested log_offset.

log_offset.description: End-of-log position after this read; pass it back as ?log_offset= to receive only new output next time.

BuildSpec.description: Contents of trtc_build_spec.json. Every build option lives here; the HTTP request carries none.

components.description: Exactly one component per builder job.

onnx.description: File name of the ONNX model inside the tar (single path segment, no directories).

strongly_typed.description: Network creation flag; precision comes from the ONNX. TensorRT 11 is strongly-typed only.

onnx_sha256.description: Optional; the build refuses a mismatching ONNX file.

external_data.description: External weight files of >2GB models: file name -> sha256, or null to skip verification. They live next to the ONNX in the tar.

profiles.description: Optimization profiles; each entry maps every dynamic input to min/opt/max shapes. Several entries build a multi-profile engine.

BuilderConfig.description: The tensorrt.IBuilderConfig, as data. Unknown keys or enum members fail the job loudly, listing exactly what this TensorRT supports. When this document is served by a TensorRT-linked builder (GET /openapi.json), every name-taking option below carries an `enum` generated from the same tables the build applies — options this TensorRT removed disappear entirely.

tactic_sources.description: TacticSource names; the engine may use only these.

ByteSize.description: Bytes, as an integer or a "512M"/"8G"-style string (K/M/G/T suffixes, binary).

Manifest.description: The spec echoed back plus build facts, per component.

onnx_sha256.description: Actual sha256 of the ONNX that was built.

external_data.description: Actual sha256 of each external data file.

gpu_name.description: Name of GPU 0, or null when no GPU/driver is visible.

compute_capability.description: CUDA compute capability of GPU 0, e.g. "8.9".

tensorrt.description: TensorRT version baked into this image (from TRTC_TENSORRT_VERSION). Clients refuse a builder that does not match their lock pin.

cache_dir.description: Engine/timing cache location on the server.
